entry.title: SAP Datasphere - Updating a source object (CDS)
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm wondering if it is possible to update a source object in a replication flow.

Currently I have a replication flow that has 1 object with 228 columns. I've extended the CDS view with 1 field so now I have 229 columns in the CDS view. However, the replication flow doesn't see this change. The only solution I have found is to add the same object again, but that means I have to manually check the projection (Mappings and filters) and all the RF settings.

See the attached screenshot as a reference. The first row is the original replication flow, the seconds row is the same object added for the second time, now displaying the correct amount of 229 columns.

entry.title: Re: Consuming data from SAP Datasphere into Power BI via ODBC connector
entry.content:
Hi @NStolze ,

I have no updates for this unfortunately.
For our project, we have settled on making the whole model up and until calculated columns (We don't have Analytical Models now) and moving those to Power BI with model import (Was the best choice for our situation) and creating measures there. Then we just schedule a daily refresh.

It's not ideal.. Hopefully we'll soon be able to make a live connection (Maybe with POI?) with using the analytical model. It really feels Datasphere is falling short on this part, since a lot of our customers use Power BI and will never switch to SAC.

One-step transfer can not be used in this scenario. You need to use two-step procedure, i.e. movement types 303 and 305.

entry.title: Re: Posting Group Journal Entry App ISSUE

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud公共版2502中有哪些AI功能可以参与Beta测试？
entry.content:
上两篇博客中我们介绍了SAP近期在S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition2502中推出的多项AI功能，以及通过限时促销免费获取SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502中的AI辅助功能的活动。 本篇将为您汇总介绍在2502中参与 Beta测试的功能，覆盖财务洞察、智能决策、搜索优化、个性化体验及错误处理等核心场景。

这些功能通过生成式AI（Generative AI）技术，将复杂流程简化，赋予用户更高效的协作与决策能力。以下我将为您解析这些创新功能，并附上参与Beta测试的快速通道。

 

Financial Business Insight Group reporting: 财务业务洞察之集团财务报表AI解析

化繁为简，让财务数据“开口说话”

核心价值： 

- 战略决策支持：通过AI解读复杂财务KPI，结合用户注释生成多维度分析，让数据背后的业务意义一目了然。 

-报告效率革命：自动生成关键数据的上下文摘要，减少人工整理时间，聚焦核心洞察。 

-协作透明化：在财务评审中整合数据与团队反馈，提升跨部门沟通效率。 

技术亮点： 

- 基于生成式AI的数据摘要与情境化KPI计算。 

- 支持用户评论与数据融合的“富文本”报告生成。 

- 提供按业务需求筛选和导航的智能工具。 

点击Beta注册链接

 

AI-assisted easy enterprise search： AI辅助智能企业搜索

用自然语言解锁全域数据搜索

核心价值

- 搜索效率飞跃：直接输入自然语言（如“显示上月未结订单”），快速定位业务对象。 

- 可信赖的AI结果：基于企业级数据的精准反馈，避免通用AI的“幻觉”风险。 

- 零学习成本：新手友好设计，一键激活即可使用。 

技术亮点： 

- 支持在SAP Fiori启动台直接进行自然语言搜索。 

- 用户端可自由启用或关闭该功能。 

- 搜索栏实时提示AI功能状态，避免混淆。 

 

点击Beta注册链接

 

AI-Assisted Smart Personalization of My Home AI智能个性化主页

打造专属数据看板

核心价值： 

- 卡片化洞察：用自然语言添加定制化数据卡片（如“显示亚太区季度销售额趋势”），实时预览效果。 

- 业务透明度提升：通过9款预置Fiori应用，快速构建关键指标仪表盘。 

技术亮点： 

- 从主页一键触发AI卡片生成流程。 

- Beta阶段支持主流业务应用的卡片创建。 

- 预览机制确保数据准确性与可视化效果。 

 

点击Beta注册链接

 

AI-Assisted Error Explanation AI辅助错误解析

从报错到解决，一步到位

核心价值： 

- 故障处理加速：自动解析技术错误并提供修复建议，减少IT依赖。 

- 无缝集成体验：在原有报错界面直接生成解释，避免跳转中断。 

技术亮点： 

- 一键触发错误原因分析与解决方案推荐。 

- 结构化呈现问题根源与操作指南。 

- 支持用户反馈情感（如点赞/改进建议），持续优化AI模型。 

 

点击Beta注册链接

Generative AI Recommendations for Situation Handling： 基于生成式AI的情况处理推荐

精准建议，让决策快人一步

核心价值：

- 职责定制化：根据用户角色与任务场景，推送针对性解决方案。 

- 响应效率提升：缩短问题处理时间，降低操作复杂度。 

技术亮点： 

- 新增“我的情况-扩展版”应用，集成AI实时推荐。 

- 通过大语言模型（LLM）整合外部数据，增强建议的全面性。 

- 基于数据上下文生成可执行的操作步骤。 

点击Beta注册链接

立即行动！

SAP为Beta参与者提供了独家技术支持和反馈通道。无论您是财务专家、IT管理员还是业务决策者，均可通过以下链接报名，抢占智能化升级的先机。立即加入Beta计划，与全球先锋用户共同定义下一代ERP的智能标准！

未来已来，AI不再仅是工具，而是成为融入业务脉动的智慧伙伴。通过SAP S/4HANA Cloud的AI赋能，企业将解锁数据潜力，让每一份洞察转化为行动力，每一步决策扎根于精准。

entry.title: Re: How to restrict MMBE and MB52 transactions with SLOC...
entry.content:
AFAIK the authorisation control in these reports does not go below plant level (check with SU24, there is no M_MATE_LGO object either).

  • The above M_MSEG_LGO authorisation, when Customizing performed, is only for stock movements (check with SU21 and SPRO documentation)
  • Ask Abaper to add such a check (also how will you hide this stock level, do you need to decrement it from higher levels, as (some) users are usually able to subtract some quantities...)

entry.title: Blog It Forward - Sandra Solís
entry.content:
Hi friends!! I am honored to start a new SAP Community series "Blog It Forward" as an exciting blogging challenge.

What can you expect? 
One blog will start a journey where you'll get to know other community members like never before. We will read about their dreams, ambitions and what fascinates them in the world of SAP. So, let’s get started and meet each other.

What can you do? 
Like and comment on your fellow community members blogs—exchange ideas, share your common interests, and have FUN!

I am Sandra Solís, an ABAP developer from Mexico.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

My expertise is in ABAP, SAP HANA and ABAP Cloud. When I was a kid, I knew computers and my curiosity to know how they work made me decide at 10 years old that I wanted to become a developer or someone working with computers. So, from high school to university, I focused entirely on learning about computers and everything related to them, especially programming. I was introduced to SAP 11 years ago, when I was looking for a job after I graduated as a Systems Engineer, a friend invited me to work with him on an SAP consultant enterprise. I did interviews and I got the job as an ABAP developer even though I didn’t know anything about it! But they trained me and that's how I became an ABAP developer. I got to know this world of SAP and I loved it. 

However, my journey wasn’t always easy, when my boss asked me to get certified I got a huge surprise when I failed the exam, not once, not twice, but 3 times! A few years later, I decided to try to get certified again, but I made some changes. First I started looking for resources and I found the SAP Community and SAP Learning. 

Second I started to draw all I read and learned, and combined with my passion for my job, I created my YouTube channel called “Mundo Abaper” (Abaper world) to share in Spanish what I was studying with visuals to people who learn visually like me who had failed their exams. When I realized people started following me, I started to create videos teaching ABAP and some tips and hacks I have learned. Thanks to this, I found my own community which I call #ABAPlovers and every day I make my videos thinking of how I can improve myself, share better content and help the ABAP community.

BTW, if you knew my YT channel and you are an #ABAPlover❤️, let me know in the comments👇😉 

A fun fact about me is that I love painting and drawing. Painting helps me stop thinking and enjoy a relaxing moment. I can paint from simple things and landscapes to portraits of people. I have a tiny sketchbook where I paint the places I have visited.

 On a fun day I painted the portraits of some SAP Developer Advocates with our favorite drink, Coffee. Yes! Coffee can also be used to paint. I delivered the paintings  to them in person and they loved it. 

I have two pet turtles, they are so big, cute and friendly, they even give me their paws.

Additionally, I love everything related to horror content like books, movies, series or real stories.  I enjoy exercising, especially dance and boxing classes. Creating content for my YouTube channel and engaging with the SAP Community are also a big part of my life and I follow a dedicated skincare and anti-aging routine as part of my commitment to self-care and well-being.

Another fun fact about me is that I won Devtoberfest 2 times! Thanks to this, the first time I went to Hana House, LA and I met advocates Thomas Jung and Michelle Moudy, and an amazing group of winners. We all went to Disney and we had a lot of fun.

The second time, I went to SAP TechEd in 2024 in West Palm Beach, FL. All the winners got to meet the SAP Developer Advocates: Rich, Josh, Thomas, Witaly, Nora and Michelle, and we spent a lot of time with all of them!! I was really happy to meet them and we all went together to Disney !!!

Also, I made a video sharing my career success with SAP you can check it out here: From a Failed Exam to a Successful Career: Sandra'... - SAP Community and I was on an episode of SAP Code talk podcast with Antonio Maradiaga!! SAP CodeTalk with Sandra Solis.

This is me so far, and I hope you're enjoying the blog! But stay with me, there's a few more below.

If you were 20 again, what would you study? 
A thousand times the same: Programming. But also, I’d focus on ABAP and dive into digital creation to enhance my video production.

What is your personal life dream?
My personal life dream is to achieve happiness and fulfillment. I want to create a balance between work and well-being. I aim to enjoy life and experience personal freedom, without being tied to conventional work norms. Above all, my dream is to continue growing emotionally and enjoy every moment of my life.

What is your professional life dream?
My professional life dream is to become the most recognized Latina, Mexican expert in the technical SAP field, contribute to the growth of the tech industry, and have the freedom to work creating impactful educational SAP content that will benefit the developer community. 

I also dream of encouraging people from Latam to explore all the SAP resources available to them, such as SAP Learning, developer tutorials, free trials, and more—many of which they might not even be aware of. I want to motivate them to participate in and attend SAP events like SAP TechEd, Devtoberfest contests, SAP Inside Tracks, and more. Additionally, I hope to inspire and encourage Latam professionals to stay active in the SAP Community and connect with the amazing Mentors, Champions, and Developer Advocates (little secret, I’m a big fan of). Can you believe that many people in Latam don’t even know about all of this?!

What do you most enjoy about the SAP Community?
All the resources they share like SAP learning, developer tutorials, the blogs and all answers the community provides. Also, all the events like SAP TechEd virtual and Devtoberfest - all of this is full of knowledge.

 

This is me so far, and I hope you have enjoyed!! 🥰

Thank you so much for taking the time to read my entire blog on SAP Community! I'm glad to hear that you found it useful/interesting. If you enjoyed it, I’d love for you to give it a like, leave a comment with your thoughts, or share it with others who might find it helpful. 

And now, I would like to Blog it Forward to my friend, and SAP Mentor: Raquel Cunha !! She is an active SAP Community contributor and passionate SAP Community.

entry.content:
Hello dear user,

The default exchange rate type used in the Balance Sheet/Income Statement app is the one defined in the country/region configuration point that you can find in SSCUI 104076 :

The exchange rate used is then derivated from the data mentioned in the Currency Exchange Rates app :

Jeremy

entry.title: SAP HANA DB Performance Tunning.
entry.content:
SAP HANA is an in-memory, column-oriented relational database management system designed for high-speed transactions and real-time analytics. While SAP HANA is optimized for performance, administrators often need to fine-tune the database to ensure smooth operations, minimize latency, and maximize efficiency.

In this blog, we will explore best practices for SAP HANA database performance fine-tuning, covering memory management, query optimization, indexing strategies, and other crucial aspects.


Understanding SAP HANA Performance Architecture 
SAP HANA’s performance relies on its in-memory architecture, where data is stored in columnar format, enabling fast read operations and parallel processing. To optimize performance, it is essential to focus on :
  Memory utilization: Efficiently managing RAM allocation for persistence, caches, and temporary storage.
  CPU optimization: Ensuring workload distribution across available cores.
  Disk I/O management: Optimizing logs, backups, and persistent storage for durability.

 

- Key Performance Fine-Tuning Strategies

1.  Memory Management Optimization: Memory is one of the most critical resources in SAP HANA, and improper usage can lead to performance bottlenecks. To monitor memory usage/memory consumption (Use HANA studio/HANA Cockpit).
SELECT * FROM M_MEMORY;

Adjust Memory Allocation to Modify global_allocation_limit based on workload requirements
ALTER SYSTEM SET GLOBAL ALLOCATION LIMIT = <value>;

2. Query Optimization:  Query optimization is a critical aspect of SAP HANA performance tuning, ensuring that SQL queries execute efficiently with minimal resource consumptions. Poorly optimized queries can lead to high memory usage, COU overload and slow response times.
 2.a - Use SQL Plan Cache Analysis: SQL Plan Cache in SAP HANA stores execution plans for queries. Analyzing it helps identify inefficient queries and optimize performance. Regularly reviewing the SQL Plan cache helps ensure efficient query execution and optimal database performance. 
                                          

SQL - SELECT TOP 10 STATEMENT_STRING, EXECUTION_COUNT,     TOTAL_EXECUTION_TIME / EXECUTION_COUNT AS AVG_EXECUTION_TIME_MS,  TOTAL_MEMORY_USED / 1024 / 1024 AS MEMORY_MB FROM M_SQL_PLAN_CACHE ORDER BY AVG_EXECUTION_TIME_MS DESC;

SQL - SELECT * FROM M_SQL_PLAN_CACHE_OVERVIEW;
(For a broader view of expensive queries)


2.b - Analysis Expensive Queries: Expensive queries consume high CPU, memory and execution time, leading to performance bottlenecks in SAP HANA. Identify and optimizing these queries can significantly improve system efficiency. Optimization strategies like Refactor complex queries, Use Indexing, Optimization Joins and Partitions Large tables. 
              

SQL - SELECT TOP 10   START_TIME,  STATEMENT_STRING,  DURATION_MICROSEC / 1000000 AS DURATION_SEC,  MEMORY_SIZE / 1024 / 1024 AS MEMORY_MB,  CPU_TIME / 1000000 AS CPU_TIME_SEC FROM M_EXPENSIVE_STATEMENTS ORDER BY DURATION_MICROSEC DESC;

 

2.b.1 - Enable Expensive State: If we don’t see enough results in M_EXPENSIVE_STATEMENTS, enable expensive statement tracing.

SQL - ALTER SYSTEM ALTER CONFIGURATION ('global.ini', 'SYSTEM') SET ('expensive_statement', threshold_duration') = '5000000' WITH RECONFIGURE;

2.c - Avoid SELECT ****(Fetch Only Required Columns): Using SELECT* in query retrieves all columns data from table, which can lead to high memory consumption, increased CPU load and slower query execution. Instead, specify only the required columns to improve performance. Like instead using SELECT * FROM customer; Use: SELECT XYZ_ID, NAME FROM customer.


2.d - Partitioning Large Tables in SAP: Partitioning is a technique used in SAP HANA to split large tables into smaller, more manageable data to improve query performance and optimize memory usage. When a table is partitioned, SAP HANA process queries more efficiently by scanning only relevant partitions instead of the entire table. Types of partitioning Hash Partitioning, Range Partitioning and Round-Robin Partitioning. 

Range Partitioning
SQL - CREATE COLUMN TABLE SALES (ID INT PRIMARY KEY, SALE_DATE DATE, AMOUNT DECIMAL(10,2))PARTITION BY RANGE (SALE_DATE) (PARTITION VALUE <= '2023-01-01', PARTITION VALUE <= '2024-01-01', PARTITION OTHERS);

HASH Partitioning
SQL - CREATE COLUMN TABLE CUSTOMER_ORDERS (ORDER_ID INT PRIMARY KEY, CUSTOMER_ID INT,  ORDER_AMOUNT DECIMAL(10,2))PARTITION BY HASH (ORDER_ID) PARTITIONS 4;

 

SELECT TABLE_NAME, PARTITION_TYPE, PARTITION_COUNT FROM M_TABLE_PARTITIONS WHERE TABLE_NAME = 'SALES';
                              (Verify Partitioning run above query to check how the table is partitioned)

3. SAP HANA Indexing Strategies: Indexing in SAP HANA helps improve query performance by reducing the amount of data scanned. However, since HANA is an in-memory database optimized for columnar storage, excessive indexing can increase memory usage without significant performance benefits.

3.a - Use Primary and Unique Indexes effectively: SAP HANA automatically creates primary key index when define a primary key. It ensures that uniqueness and speed up lookup but may slow down inserts. Example creating a Primary index

SQL - CREATE TABLE Customers (CustomerID INT PRIMARY KEY, NAME NVARCHAR(50), CITY NVARCHAR(50));

3.b - Use Unique Indexes: Similar to primary key but allow null values. Use when need uniqueness but don’t want it as the primary key. Example creating a Unique Index

SQL – CREATE UNIQUE INDEX UQ_Email ON CUSTOMERS(EMAILS);

  1. c – Avoid Unnecessary Secondary Indexes: SAP HANA columnar storage already provide efficient indexing for most queries. Create Secondary indexes only when necessary, such as for frequently filtered queries on non-primary key columns.

 3.d – Monitor and Drop Unused Index: Monitoring and cleaning up unused indexes, SAP HANA performance and memory efficiency can be optimized. Avoid keeping unnecessary indexes that increase memory consumption. Identify unused indexes, check if indexes are rarely or never used to save memory.

SQL: SELECT TABLE_NAME, INDEX_NAME, INDEX_TYPE, RECORD_COUNT FROM M_INDEXES WHERE RECORD_COUNT = 0;

Drop unused index SQL - DROP INDEX IDX_CITY;

3.e – Optimize Joins with Indexes: To improve joins performance in SAP HANA, indexing foreign key columns is essential. Creating index on frequently joined columns reduces full table scans and speeds up query execution. Additionally, ensuring primary or unique indexes exist on joined columns enhances efficiency. However, over indexing should be avoided as it can slow down insert and update operations. Indexing frequently used joins leads to faster query execution and better memory management in database.  

3.d – Partition Large Indexes for Better Performance: Partitioning large tables helps SAP HANA distribute data across multiple processing units, improving query performance and memory management. When a table has a massive dataset, indexing alone may not be sufficient. By partitioning the index, queries can process data in parallel, reducing execution time. Two very common types of partitioning for large Indexes. 1) Hash Partitioning, 2) Range Partitioning.

SQL - SELECT TABLE_NAME, INDEX_NAME, RECORD_COUNT, MEMORY_SIZE_IN_TOTAL / 1024 / 1024 AS MEMORY_MB FROM M_INDEXES WHERE MEMORY_SIZE_IN_TOTAL > 1024 * 1024 * 1024  -- Filters indexes larger than 1GB
ORDER BY MEMORY_MB DESC;
                                                            (To check the size of index)

4. Parallel Processing and Thread Optimization: SAP HANA uses parallel processing to maximize CPU utilization and improve query performance. It distributes workload across multiple threads, leveraging multiple core CPUs and columnar storage for efficient execution.

              - Parallel Query Execution Distributes query execution across multiple threads.
              - Parallel Index scans improve performance when scanning large indexes datasets.
              - Parallel Background tasks include data loads, replication and garbage collection.
              - Parallel table processing, each partition is processed by different threads for efficiency.             

4.a – Checking thread usage & Parallel execution to analysis SAP HANA parallel processing usage, use the below query
              SQL - SELECT * FROM M_SERVICE_THREADS WHERE STATE!=’idle’;

4.b - Check CPU & Thread Utilization by SQL Statements. Identifies queries consuming high CPU and using multiple threads.

SQL - SELECT STATEMENT_STRING, THREAD_COUNT, CPU_TIME, EXECUTION_COUNT FROM M_SQL_PLAN_CACHE ORDER BY CPU_TIME DESC LIMIT 10;

4.c – Enable parallel execution for queries and ensure parallel query execution is thread on.

SQL- ALTER SYSTEM ALTER CONFIGURATION ('global.ini', 'SYSTEM') SET ('parallel_execution', 'enabled') = 'true' WITH RECONFIGURE;

4.d – Increase maximum parallel threads per query. Default is 5, increasing can improve performance for complex workloads

SQL- ALTER SYSTEM ALTER CONFIGURATION ('global.ini', 'SYSTEM') SET ('execution', 'max_concurrent_statements') = '10' WITH RECONFIGURE;

4.e – Optimize memory allocation for threads to adjust the number of threads per service. It helps allocate optimal threads for complex workload.

SQL- ALTER SYSTEM ALTER CONFIGURATION ('indexserver.ini', 'SYSTEM') SET ('threads', 'max_concurrency') = 'value' WITH RECONFIGURE;

4.f - Monitoring parallel performance to identify long running queries that may need parallel tuning. To check parallel execution efficiency run the following query.

              SQL- SELECT*FROM M_EXPENSIVE_STATEMENTS ORDER BY DURATION_MICROSEC DESC;

5. Disk I/O Optimization: SAP HANA is an in-memory database, but it still relies on disk storage for logging, persistence and backup. Optimizing disk I/O is critical to ensure high performance, reduce latency and avoid bottlenecks. The primary goal of disk I/O optimization is to maximize throughput and minimize latency by optimizing storage configuration, filesystem settings and system parameters.

5.1 – Linux Kernel & I/O schedulers tunning: Use the noop or deadline I/O scheduler for better disk performance.
                 echo noop > /sys/block/sdX/queue/scheduler
-- Adjust kernel parameters in /etc/sysctl.conf
                  vm.dirty_background_ratio=5
                  vm.dirty_ratio=5
                  vm.swappiness=0
                  vm.overcommit_memory=2

5.b – Optimizing SAP HANA Log Volume Size: The log volume is critical because it stores all transactions logs before they are written to the data volume. To optimize log volume performance
              - Use SSD for log volumes to ensure high throughput and low latency
              - Avoid using shared storage unless it is high-performance with low latency.
              - Keep the log volume separately from data volumes to prevent I/O contention.
              - SAP recommends a log volume size of at least 4 times of log buffer size, if you frequently see log segment switches due to space constraints, increase the log volume size

5.c – Adjusting log buffer size for better performance: The log buffer temporarily stores transaction logs before writing them to the log volume. Optimizing can reduce the number of I/O operations. Restart SAP HANA database services for changes to take effect.

              SQL- SELECT VALUE FROM M_INIFILE_CONTENTS WHERE KEY =’logbuffer_size’;

  • Increase log buffer size based on requirement. Default value is 32 MB and SAP recommended 64 MB or 128 MB depending on transaction workload. To modify

SQL – ALTER SYSTEM ALTER CONFIGURATION (‘global.ini’, ‘SYSTEM’) SET (‘persistence’, ‘logbuffer_size’) = ‘128MB’ WITH RECONFIGURE;  

- Note: If WAIT_TIME is high, increase logbuffer_size and check disk performance.

5.d - Additional Performance Tunning
              - Enable Asynchronous I/O for log writing (enable_async_io = on)
              - Ensure fast log writes, if log_write_duration is high, optimize storage or increase log buffer.
              SQL -  SELECT*FROM M_LOG_WRITTING_STATISTICS;

Thanks,
Abhijeet Chakraborty

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to check when the last execution of a transaction...
entry.content:
To check the last use of a transaction code, you can use the transaction STAT/STAD.

As for checking each transaction start (date, time, user, and other details) including failed/locked transactions in addition to successful attempts, then the best way would be to use the Security Audit Log (SAL) in transaction SM20. This would need to be configured in SM19 first before it can start recording such events. This is detailed further in SAP KBA 0003352573.

There would also be more information on the usage (e.g. amount of time used) in transaction ST03/ST03n.

entry.title: Re: SAP Mobile Start fails to load App
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I found a workaround to add the Build App to Work Zone by creating an app in the local channel within Work Zone, instead of using the standard approach for adding an app deployed in CF via Content Explorer → HTML5 Repository (here is also a blog post confirming that the standard approach should work.)

By creating the app (tile) in Work Zone and configuring it with the URL, semantic object, and action, the app opens correctly in Mobile Start. However, I wanted to use the standard method by adding the app from the Content Explorer.

I reached out to SAP to check if this issue would be fixed, and here’s their response:

I am part of the Build Apps development team, and I wanted to provide an update about your case.

We are aware of a problem preventing Mobile Start from loading Build Apps applications.

It is not necessary to add Build Apps applications through the HTML5 Content Provider, although I do acknowledge the convenience tradeoff. The technical explanation, as you may guess, is that Build Apps applications are added to Work Zone as simple URL tiles, so there is no need to synchronize or update them through the Content Provider – they always load the latest deployed version.

We have now made this the official way to add our apps to Work Zone: SAP Help Guide.

So, what previously worked is now no longer functioning. SAP has updated the official documentation to reflect this new approach.

Thank you,
Diego

entry.title: F0719 Plan Customer Projects, "Service Price List" should be read-only
entry.content:
We are faced with the following problem: The project managers have been given a customer-specific application role based on the role template SAP_BR_PROJECT_MANAGER_PROF to plan customer projects. This allows the project manager to open the “Plan Customer Projects” app.

 

 

 

If the project manager goes into edit mode by clicking “Edit”, they can change their customer project.

 

 

 

 

 

Below the “Billing” tab, he can navigate to the “Service Price List” function. If he navigate there...

 

 

 

 

 

 

... they can change project-specific prices there. In the URL address you can see that you are in a sub-functionality (“RateCard”). But you are still in the same app.

 

 

 

 

After changing the price, the project managers can return to the main screen.

 

 

 

There they can save their price change with “Save”. If you call up “Service Price List” again, you will see the changed price there. The saving was effective.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Our requirement now is that the project managers can only see the specific project prices, but cannot change them. These price changes are reserved for the management. How can we switch off the change function for the project managers specifically for the price list, even though it is the same app?

entry.title: Composable Storefront Multi-country under one domain name
entry.content:
Introduction

This article will explain shortly how to setup Composable Storefront in order to get multi-country website with unique domain name. 

Idea is to have this kind of URL path pattern : https://<company.com>/<country>/<language>

Commerce BackOffice

Content Catalog

First thing is to setup a multi-country content catalog structure for supporting this website. Parent content catalog will contain global website data where Childs content catalog will contain specific country content. Then, you will need to create one child content catalog for each country.

CMSSite

Now, you need to create a CMSSite for each country. CMSSite makes the link with your content catalog and your product catalog. We could imagine to have global product catalog shared by all countries or to create a dedicated product catalog for each country. This design decision should be made according your organisation.

Another aspect is the BaseStore. This object represents your legal entity that will receive orders. Again, we could imagine to have a global BaseStore or a dedicated BaseStore by country. 

BaseSite mapping

After you created all your CMSSites, you have now to indicate for Composable Storefront which BaseSite/CMSite must be loaded regarding URL pattern.

You should find in CMSSite item a property name URLPatterns

In our design, you must add this following pattern/regex rule at the beginning. For example, Electronics UK will have 

 

(?i)^https?://www.electronics.com/uk+(|/.*|\\?.*)$

 

 

BaseSite URL Encoding

You need also to indicate for Composable Storefront how URL path should be computed according site context. For that, you need to update the property URL encoding attributes

BaseSite Default Country

In native default country doesn't exist at BaseSite level. You will need to extend data model for adding this property as Base Configuration. Then this new value can be transmitted to Composable Storefront through OCC API BaseSite : /occ/v2/basesites

Composable Storefront

Site Context

Composable Storefront needs to compute correctly new URL path according BaseSite setting. Country parameter is not native and it must be introduced. For that, you have to customize SiteContextConfigInitializer 

https://github.com/SAP/spartacus/blob/develop/projects/core/src/site-context/config/config-loader/site-context-config-initializer.ts

You can review the following method getConfig(source: BaseSite): SiteContextConfig

Multi Country Selector Component

Last optional step is to introduce a new component that will help your customer to switch from a country to another.

First, you need to create a CMSFlexComponent in BackOffice with flexType as MulticountrySelectorComponent

In Composable Storefront side, you need to create this new component and map this Angular component to Commerce CMS Component. For that, you just have to declare the following mapping

 

// CMS Component mapping
  // with CMSFlexComponent flexType = MulticountrySelectorComponent
  providers: [
    provideDefaultConfig(<CmsConfig>{
      cmsComponents: {
        MulticountrySelectorComponent: {
          component: MulticountrySelectorComponent,
        },
      },
    }),]

 

In side Angular Component, you can use BaseSiteService to get data from OCC BaseSite call and do all the rendering logic

 

import { BaseSiteService } from '@spartacus/core';

 

Then in MulticountrySelectorComponent template, you can use simple <a href> to do redirect toward other country website.

In this context, each country will be considered as different SPA. It is why we cannot use Angular <a routerLink>

Conclusion

SAP Commerce Cloud and Composable Storefront are very flexible. And with few adaptions, we can easily create multi-country website. 

If you need to enter in more detail or/and need more support for your business, please don't hesitate to contact SAP Expert Services.

We can can help you to design the best solution according your business needs.

entry.title: Re: How to get the mapped fieldnames from a result value...
entry.content:
READ ENTITY IN LOCAL MODE zi_cubco6
      ALL FIELDS WITH CORRESPONDING #( keys )
      RESULT DATA(cubco6_entities).

    DATA table_data TYPE TABLE OF zcubcot6 WITH EMPTY KEY.
    table_data = CORRESPONDING #( cubco6_entities MAPPING FROM ENTITY ).

 

Prerequisites: in the BDEF of entity zi_cubco6 there is a "mapping for zcubcot6"
See also https://help.sap.com/doc/abapdocu_latest_index_htm/latest/en-US/abapeml_corresponding.htm

entry.title: Re: Remove the approval for purchase requisitions
entry.content:
Hello @spring_sap2023 

The status comes from Release Procedure (MM-PUR-GF) You can't just "remove" the statuts. As the purchase requisition is relevant for a release procedure, it needs to be released - Releasing Purchase Requisitions

If those purchase requisitions should not be realy released, the release procedure configuration needs to be updated - SPRO: Materials Management -> Purchasing -> Purchase Requisition -> Release Procedure -> ...

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Need help to optimize/compact code with abap 7.4 syntax
entry.content:
Hi,

I have this code(optimized for copy/paste):

 

 

 

    TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_ctr,
             doc_num TYPE string,
             doc_soc TYPE string,
             doc_typ TYPE string,
           END OF ty_ctr.

    TYPES tty_ctr TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_ctr WITH DEFAULT KEY.

    CONSTANTS c_aq      TYPE string VALUE 'AQ'.
    CONSTANTS c_def     TYPE string VALUE 'ZZZZ'.

    DATA lt_ctr_temp2   TYPE tty_ctr.

    DATA(lt_ctr_ret) = VALUE tty_ctr( ( doc_num = '123' doc_soc = 'ZZ01' doc_typ = 'AQ' )
                                      ( doc_num = '123' doc_soc = 'ZZ02' doc_typ = 'AQ' )
                                      ( doc_num = '234' doc_soc = 'ZZ03' doc_typ = 'AQ' )
                                      ( doc_num = '567' doc_soc = 'ZZ04' doc_typ = 'ZZ' ) ).

    LOOP AT lt_ctr_ret INTO DATA(ls_ctr_ret).
      DATA(lt_ctr_temp1) =  VALUE tty_ctr( FOR ls_ctr IN lt_ctr_ret WHERE ( doc_num = ls_ctr_ret-doc_num AND doc_typ = c_aq ) ( ls_ctr ) ).
      DELETE lt_ctr_ret WHERE doc_num = ls_ctr_ret-doc_num AND doc_typ = c_aq.
      IF lines( lt_ctr_temp1 ) > 1.
        SORT lt_ctr_temp1 BY doc_num.
        DELETE ADJACENT DUPLICATES FROM lt_ctr_temp1 COMPARING doc_num.
        DATA(ls_ctr_soc) = VALUE ty_ctr( doc_soc = c_def ).
        MODIFY lt_ctr_temp1 FROM ls_ctr_soc TRANSPORTING doc_soc WHERE doc_num EQ ls_ctr_ret-doc_num.
      ENDIF.
      APPEND LINES OF lt_ctr_temp1 TO lt_ctr_temp2.
    ENDLOOP.
    APPEND LINES OF lt_ctr_temp2 TO lt_ctr_ret.

 

 

 

This code simply search for document number with doc type 'AQ'.

If there are 'AQ' documents with multiple society the result is a single row with society 'ZZZZ'.

lt_ctr_ret is the input and result table.

Input:

Result:

I need to know if there is a way tu compact and optimize this code with abap 7.4/7.5x syntax.

Thank you.

entry.title: Step-by-Step Guide: Adding Your Domain to Trusted Domains in SAP IAS
entry.content:
When using embedded iframes or overlays in an application, which authenticates against the Identity Authentication service, the error detailed in the screenshot below is displayed when trying to log in via the domain of the overlay.

Possible error messages  are displayed:

  • "Your domain is not trusted. Please contact your system administrator."
  • "Your request failed because the page you have to be redirected to is not trusted. Please contact your system administrator."

To fix issue - Configure Trusted Domains

Kindly ask your SAP IAS Admin to follow the below steps to fix the issue

  1. Add the necessary domain(s) to the IAS tenant's trust settings as described in Configure Trusted Domains
  2. Enable third-party cookies in the browser (check the respective browser's documentation);
  3. Disable "Prevent cross-site tracking" or "Do not track" options in the browser (the terminology may vary; check the respective browser's documentation).

entry.title: Re: Difference between Assignment of People and Assignmen...
entry.content:
Hi Riyal,

Assign people is based on the operation split and identifies the executing technicians which could be multiple – with one-to-many relationships. The person responsible is the one who has true ownership of the operation or order, and typically the one entering the time confirmations. Depending on your process, one or both could be relevant for you.

Here are some links which go into more detail. Furthermore, a roadmap link where we plan to support assigning a person responsible (in addition to assign people) from the maintenance scheduling board.

Link Person Responsible: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/e72f747389b340229f7fa343975bfa57/f4b08e2549744c3fb2bea93b9fabc94e.html?locale=en-US&q=person+responsible

Link Assign People: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/e72f747389b340229f7fa343975bfa57/1163f6e5700244fab2d6a3364ce3f988.html?locale=en-US&q=assign+people

Roadmap link: https://roadmaps.sap.com/board?range=CURRENT-LAST&BC=089E017A62AB1ED9BDE754AB9FFEC0DD#Q1%202025;INNO=935C1AA1B4311EDF97F3B3DE494B4A3F

Kind regards,
Ryan

entry.title: Re: SAP Event Mesh: Event Connector to Microsoft Azure to go Beta
entry.content:
Hi @vbalko-claimate, 

To my understanding there are in the end two/three approaches:

1. SAP Event Mesh or EMIS via CPI to Event Grid

2.  SAP Event Mesh via Webhooks to Event Grid (I believe that there is a blog by Holger Bruchelt on this).

3. Since you have mentioned Event Hub: I would assume that Event Hub via CPI to Event Grid will become an option as well. 

Best,

Karsten

entry.title: Re: Can we still edit a Transferred Sales Order from C4C...
entry.content:
Hi @mracantonius1 

It very much depends on the integration flow you're using and the fact you're using S/4 HANA in cloud version or not. However, the capability of C4C to edit transferred orders is generally limited and may be restricted based on the integration settings or middleware, and it primarily depends on the status and the integration setup. Complex changes, especially around the item-level details or pricing, typically require backend intervention in S/4HANA or ECC using transaction VA02.

Can you provide more details about your integration scenario?

entry.title: ABAP RAP managed scenario, shortdump in case of duplicate key
entry.content:
Hi there,

I try to build a RAP managed scenario around a simple table. The table consists of multiple key fields. One of these fields, I want to control the numbering. So I implemented a method for this.

Now I want to create a bunch of records. The MODIFY statement works good the first time and data are stored in table. When I run the code again, this will lead to duplicate records.

My expectation is that this situation either gets reported by MODIFY ENTITY statement or by COMMIT ENTITIES. 

The result is like this. MODIFY ENTITIES reports no error. COMMIT ENTITIES raises a shortdump. I was expecting that it will fill the FAILED or REPORTED, but this is not true.

Here a simplified example of my table

 

@EndUserText.label : 'My table'
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #A
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #RESTRICTED
define table zmy_table {

  key client           : abap.clnt not null;
  key order            : aufnr not null;
  key item             : int4 not null;
  Material             : matnr;

}

 

CDS View

 

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'My Table'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@ObjectModel.usageType:{
    serviceQuality: #X,
    sizeCategory: #S,
    dataClass: #MIXED
}
define root view entity zi_my_table as select from zmy_table
{
    key order as Order,
    key item as Item,
    material as Material
}

 

Behavior

 

managed implementation in class zbp_my_class unique;
strict ( 2 );

define behavior for zi_my_table alias MyTable
persistent table zmy_table
lock master
authorization master ( instance )
early numbering
//etag master <field_name>
{
  create;
  update;
  delete;


    field( readonly : update )
    order,
    item;

  field( mandatory : create )
  Material;

  mapping for zmy_table
  {
    Order = order;
    Item = item;
    Material = material;
   }

}

 

 Behavior class

 

CLASS lhc_zi_my_table DEFINITION INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_handler.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
    METHODS get_instance_authorizations FOR INSTANCE AUTHORIZATION
      IMPORTING keys REQUEST requested_authorizations FOR myTable RESULT result.

    METHODS earlynumbering_create FOR NUMBERING
      IMPORTING entities FOR CREATE myTable.

ENDCLASS.


CLASS lhc_zi_my_table IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD get_instance_authorizations.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD earlynumbering_create.
    DATA(entities_wo_id) = entities.

    SORT entities_wo_id BY order
                           material.

    LOOP AT entities_wo_id ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<new_entity>).
      " first check the largest ID used so far for given entry

      SELECT MAX( item ) FROM @mapped-myTable AS existing
        WHERE order = @<new_entity>-order
        INTO @DATA(max_item).

        <new_entity>-Item = max_item + 1.

      APPEND VALUE #( %cid = <new_entity>-%cid
                      %key = <new_entity>-%key )
             TO mapped-myTable.

    ENDLOOP.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

 

 

Here I try to add new records. As long as an order has not been added so far, everything works. Once the order already exists, my code of item determination will lead to duplicate records. 

 

    MODIFY ENTITIES OF zi_my_table
           ENTITY MyTable
           CREATE AUTO FILL CID WITH VALUE #(
               ( Order = '123456789
                 Material = 'ABCDEF' ) 
               ( Order = '123456789
                 Material = 'XYZABC' )
)

           MAPPED DATA(mapped_data)
           FAILED DATA(failed_records)
           REPORTED DATA(reported_records).

" This works for the first time but dump in case of additional execution.
    IF failed_records IS INITIAL AND reported_records IS INITIAL.
      COMMIT ENTITIES RESPONSES
             FAILED DATA(failed_commit)
             REPORTED DATA(reported_commit).

    ENDIF.

 

I'm aware of this situation but I really wonder why this does result in a shortdump instead of simple report failed records

entry.title: Re: Creation of custom roles for Cloud ALM (eg. restrict...
entry.content:
Hi Luigi,

KBA 3499177 has information about the pre-built role collections s available for Implementation functionality in SAP Cloud ALM.

You can examine the role collections in the BTP cockpit to find the individual role objects assigned to them. However, you'll quickly find that most of the Project and Feature related role collections have single role objects. Here's an example, the "Project Member" role collection just has one role object assigned to it, which is responsible for all of its permissions.

Because the role collection permissions are governed by single role objects, this means that the permission control is not granular. There is currently no way to separate the predetermined responsibilities and permissions for the "project member" and then create a custom "project member" role collection with only a subset of those responsibilities and permissions. So custom SAP Cloud ALM roles for Implementation are only "additive", not "subtractive".

Thanks and regards, 
John

entry.title: Re: How to get the mapped fieldnames from a result value...
entry.content: @handri_gunawanI cannot explain the error message "No mapping is defined...". If the mapping from the create structure is fine you can try to create a variable like "DATA change TYPE REQUEST FOR CHANGE ZI_Header_S." and then first move the data from the read structure to the change structure with corresponding #( ) and then move the data from the change structure to the table.

entry.title: Cost Center Hierarchy consolidation and replication
entry.content:
For a two-tier ERP setup. While checking the Master Data replication in general, MDI is the recommended solution for replicating between SAP and Non-SAP applications. 

Two-tier ERP Master Data Replication Options - SAP Community

In my case, we have a Non-SAP HCM application that is receiving the Cost Center Hierarchy and Bank Master from SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud viz PI/PO at present . However with two-tier setup now that Cost Center Hierarchy and Bank Master would be maintained separately in S/4HANA Private Cloud and S/4HANA Public cloud tenants whereas the Non-SAP HCM application needs Cost Center Hierarchy and Bank Master from both applications as consolidated in a single structure. 

1. Can MDI handle such consolidation of Cost Center Hierarchy and bank master from S/4HANA Private Cloud and S/4HANA Public cloud tenants and replicate as a single structure to Non-SAP HCM application PULLED viz Cloud Integration of course?

ODM Model Avaialable : 2. Are those ODM object relevant and only available to meet Cost Center Hierarchy and Bank Master Data ?

sap.odm.finance.costobject.CostCenter

sap.odm.finance.bank.Bank

3. Is these integration pattern still relevant and valid ?

Replicate Cost Centers from SAP S4HANA Cloud to Workday | IFlow | SAP Business Accelerator Hub

Replicate Cost Centers from SAP S4HANA to Workday | IFlow | SAP Business Accelerator Hub

 

Thanks & Regards,

Karthick Srinivasan

entry.title: Your opportunity to Influence SAP: SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Projects 2025-1 for SAP Concur
entry.content:
Connect financial data and take control of spending. By automating spending processes your business can run efficiently anywhere, anytime, and under any circumstances.

Scroll through the list of SAP Customer Engagement Initiative projects for SAP Concur and get informed about the planned new features and functions.

And don’t forget to register for one of the projects until March 28, 2025

SAP Concur Solutions projects for registration:

 

SAP Concur Solutions - Customer Managed Keys for granular control over data

SAP Concur - Dynamic Card Management – Virtual Credit Cards

SAP Concur Solutions - Web Notifications Center and Preferences

SAP Concur Mobile app and the sign-in user experience

SAP Concur Solutions – All About E-Receipts

SAP Concur Solutions - Sponsored Guest Feature

SAP Concur - AI Managed Expense Reports

SAP Concur Expense - Web Services for Financial Integration

 

Visit the Customer Influence website on influence.sap.com to register.

If you are also interested in other CEI projects that are currently open for registration, see here the full list.

Thank you in advance for your participation!

 

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.
